output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <filesystem>
#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace test_design_eas_database {

  struct Student {
    [sqlgen::PrimaryKey]
    [sqlgen::NotNull]
    sqlgen::Col<"SNo", std::string, Type="VARCHAR(10)"> SNo;

    [sqlgen::NotNull]
    sqlgen::Col<"SName", std::string, Type="VARCHAR(30)"> SName;

    sqlgen::Col<"Sex", int> Sex = 1;

    sqlgen::Col<"Birthday", std::string, Type="DATETIME"> Birthday;

    sqlgen::Col<"Major", int> Major;

    sqlgen::Col<"Scorses", float> Scorses = 0.0f;

    sqlgen::Col<"Remark", std::string> Remark;
  };

  struct Course {
    [sqlgen::PrimaryKey]
    [sqlgen::AutoIncrement]
    sqlgen::Col<"CID", int> CID;

    [sqlgen::NotNull]
    sqlgen::Col<"CName", std::string, Type="VARCHAR(30)"> CName;

    sqlgen::Col<"Term", int> Term = 1;

    sqlgen::Col<"CTime", int> CTime;

    [sqlgen::NotNull]
    sqlgen::Col<"Credit", float> Credit = 0.0f;
  };

  struct Score {
    [sqlgen::ForeignKey(table = "Student", column = "SNo")]
    [sqlgen::PrimaryKey]
    sqlgen::Col<"SNo", std::string, Type="VARCHAR(10)"> SNo;

    [sqlgen::ForeignKey(table = "Course", column = "CID")]
    [sqlgen::PrimaryKey]
    sqlgen::Col<"CID", int> CID;

    sqlgen::Col<"Score", float> Score;
  };

  void design_eas_database() {
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();
    sqlgen::create_table<Student>(conn, true);
    sqlgen::create_table<Course>(conn, true);
    sqlgen::create_table<Score>(conn, true);
  }

}
```</answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <optional>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace query_search_books_by_criteria {

struct Book {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> book_id;
  std::string title;
  std::string author;
  int year;
  std::string genre;
  double price;
};

struct BookFilters {
  std::optional<std::string> title_keyword;
  std::optional<int> min_year;
  std::optional<std::string> genre;
  std::optional<double> max_price;
};

/**
 * Function: Search for books based on multiple optional criteria.
 * 
 * @param filters Optional filters for title, year, genre, and price
 * @return List of books matching the criteria
 */
std::vector<Book> query_search_books_by_criteria(const BookFilters& filters) {
  // Create a connection to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect();
  if (!conn_result) {
    // Since the function doesn't return an error, we throw an exception on failure
    throw std::runtime_error("Failed to connect to database");
  }
  auto conn = conn_result.value();

  // Build the condition dynamically
  auto condition = sqlgen::where(true); // Start with a true condition

  if (filters.title_keyword) {
    condition = condition && (sqlgen::col<"title">() % *filters.title_keyword);
  }

  if (filters.min_year) {
    condition = condition && (sqlgen::col<"year">() >= *filters.min_year);
  }

  if (filters.genre) {
    condition = condition && (sqlgen::col<"genre">() == *filters.genre);
  }

  if (filters.max_price) {
    condition = condition && (sqlgen::col<"price">() <= *filters.max_price);
  }

  // Build the query
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Book> | sqlgen::where(condition);

  // Execute the query and return the results
  return query(conn).value();
}

}  // namespace query_search_books_by_criteria
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>

namespace schema_migration_add_column {

struct User {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> user_id;
  std::string username;
};

struct UserAfter {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> user_id;
  std::string username;
  std::string phone_number;
  bool is_active;
};

/**
 * Function: Perform schema migration to add new columns.
 * 
 * @return true if migration succeeds, false otherwise
 */
bool migration_add_column() {
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return false;
  }
  auto conn = *conn_result;

  const std::string sql1 = "ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN phone_number VARCHAR(20);";
  const std::string sql2 = "ALTER TABLE User ADD COLUMN is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1;";

  auto result1 = conn->execute(sql1);
  if (!result1) {
    return false;
  }

  auto result2 = conn->execute(sql2);
  if (!result2) {
    return false;
  }

  return true;
}

} // namespace schema_migration_add_column

struct Account {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> account_id;

};

bool transfer_funds(int from_account_id, int to_account_id, double amount) {
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;

    auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    if (!conn_result) return false;

    auto conn = conn_result.value();

    auto begin_result = conn.begin_transaction();
    if (!begin_result) return false;

    auto update_from = sqlgen::update<Account> 
        .set("balance"_c = "balance"_c - amount)
        .where("account_id"_c == from_account_id && "balance"_c >= amount);

    auto update_from_result = conn.execute(update_from);

        return false;
    }

    if (!update_to_result) {
        conn.rollback();
        return false;
    }

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <ranges>
#include <rfl.hpp>
#include <rfl/json.hpp>
#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int stock;
};

struct Sale {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int, sqlgen::auto_incr> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  double amount;
};

struct SalesSummary {
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  std::string product_name;
  int total_quantity;
  double total_revenue;
};

/**
 * Function: Create a view SalesSummary that aggregates sales by product.
 * 
 * @return true if view creation succeeds, false otherwise
 */
bool create_view_sales_summary() {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  if (!conn_result) {
    return false;
  }
  auto conn = *conn_result;

  // Build the SELECT query for the view
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Sale, Product>("s", "p")
      .field("p.name", "product_name")
      .sum("s.quantity").as("total_quantity")
      .sum("s.amount").as("total_revenue")
      .where("s.product_id = p.product_id")
      .group_by("p.name");

  // Create the view with IF NOT EXISTS
  auto create_view_stmt = sqlgen::create_as<SalesSummary>(query).if_not_exists();

  // Convert to SQL and execute
  std::string sql = sqlgen::to_sql(create_view_stmt);
  return conn->execute(sql).is_success();
}

}  // namespace schema_create_view_sales_summary
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace replace_text_in_notes {

struct Note {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string content;
};

struct UpdatedNote {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> note_id;
  std::string updated_content;
};

/**
 * description: replace_text_in_notes.
 * This function demonstrates how to replace text in notes using sqlgen update API.
 * It replaces all occurrences of "foo" with "bar" in the content column of the Note table.
 * Returns: std::vector<UpdatedNote>.
 */
std::vector<UpdatedNote> replace_text_in_notes() {
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();
  
  // Perform the update operation: replace "foo" with "bar" in content
  auto update_result = (conn | sqlgen::update<Note>(sqlgen::set(Note::content = sqlgen::replace(Note::content, "foo", "bar")))).value();
  
  // Read the updated notes from the database
  auto notes = (update_result | sqlgen::read<std::vector<Note>>).value();
  
  // Convert the notes to the UpdatedNote format
  std::vector<UpdatedNote> updated_notes;
  for (const auto& note : notes) {
    updated_notes.push_back({note.note_id, note.content});
  }
  
  return updated_notes;
}

}  // namespace replace_text_in_notes

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read {

struct Inventory {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  std::string name;
  int quantity;
  std::string location;
};

/**
 * description: inventory_bulk_write_and_read.
 * This function demonstrates how to perform bulk write and read operations on an inventory table.
 * It first drops and recreates the table, then writes the given items to the table.
 * Finally, it reads back the items with quantity > 50, orders them by quantity in descending order,
 * and returns true if at least one item is found.
 * Returns: bool.
 */
bool inventory_bulk_write_and_read(const std::vector<Inventory>& items) {
    // Connect to the database
    auto conn_result = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    if (!conn_result) {
        return false;
    }
    auto conn = *conn_result;

    // Drop the table if it exists
    auto drop_result = (sqlgen::drop<Inventory> | sqlgen::if_exists)(conn);
    if (!drop_result) {
        return false;
    }

    // Create the table using a CREATE TABLE statement
    const std::string create_table_sql = 
        "CREATE TABLE Inventory (item_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, quantity INTEGER NOT NULL, location VARCHAR(50))";
    auto create_result = conn->execute(create_table_sql);
    if (!create_result) {
        return false;
    }

    // Write the items
    auto write_result = conn->write(items.begin(), items.end());
    if (!write_result) {
        return false;
    }

    // Read the items with condition and ordering
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;

    auto read_result = (sqlgen::read<std::vector<Inventory>> 
        | sqlgen::where("quantity"_c > 50)
        | sqlgen::order_by("quantity"_c.desc()))(conn);

    if (!read_result) {
        return false;
    }

    return !read_result->empty();
}

}  // namespace inventory_bulk_write_and_read
```</answer>

  std::string product_name;
  std::string category;
  double total_sales;
};

        .limit(limit_count)
        .to<std::vector<TopProduct>>;

    return conn->template read<std::vector<TopProduct>>(query).value();
}

}  // namespace get_top_products

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace weekday_sales_analysis {

struct Sales {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> sale_id;
  int product_id;
  int quantity;
  sqlgen::Timestamp<"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"> sale_time;
};

struct WeekdayStats {
  int weekday;
  int total_quantity;
};

/**
 * Description: This function analyzes sales data to calculate total quantity sold for each weekday.
 * Returns: std::vector<WeekdayStats>.
 */
std::vector<WeekdayStats> weekday_sales_analysis() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Sales>(sqlgen::extract(sqlgen::DayOfWeek, "sale_time"_c).as("weekday"), sqlgen::sum("quantity"_c).as("total_quantity"))
             .group_by("weekday");
  return query(conn).read<std::vector<WeekdayStats>>;
}

}  // namespace weekday_sales_analysis
```</answer>
